data.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child fe57 "Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client (served through the production path)"), 4-bucket per parent criterion, cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing — no G3 coverage finding attributable to this child; no uncovered parent criterion remains across the roster.

OWNED / CONTRIBUTED by THIS child (fe57 = the foundation: scrubbed real-Jira fixtures + FakeAcliClient + live-gated capture/scrub script + re-capture doc):
- Parent AC "One round-trip contract test exercises fetcher → snapshot → differs → applier through the production path, USING FIXTURES DERIVED FROM REAL JIRA PAYLOADS" → fe57 OWNS the "fixtures derived from real Jira payloads" half: fe57 AC1 (tests/fixtures/jira/*.json hold scrubbed real Jira payloads for search+comment+issuelinks+parent; no tokens/emails/accountIds) + AC2 (FakeAcliClient serves them through the production fetch_snapshot enrichment with NO shape transformation) + AC4 (fixtures consumed only via the production path, no hand-reshaping). The round-trip TEST itself is correctly delegated to keystone 8937 (story B, which declares depends_on fe57). FULLY covered jointly (fixtures → fe57; test → 8937).
- Parent SC "Producer (fetcher + enrichment) and consumers (differs/appliers) cannot drift on snapshot shape without a test failing" → fe57 OWNS the honesty-of-input half: hermetic-but-honest fixtures served unmassaged through the production path remove the bug-0ee6/3f04 root cause (differs fed hand-built shapes the real fetcher never emits). Complemented by 8937 (schema SSOT + joint round-trip) and 59ef (verified-fake fidelity). CONTRIBUTED.
- Parent SC "New tests assert behavior/contract … the suite stays lean; zero change-detector tests added" → fe57 AC3 (live-gated capture + documented re-capture cadence "so fixtures stay honest and do not rot into change-detectors"). CONTRIBUTED.

DELEGATION — parent criteria fe57 does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; fe57's declared OUT items (the contract test → story B/8937; the verified-fake honesty check → story C/59ef) are consistent with the roster:
- AC "SSOT snapshot-entry schema module" → 8937 AC1.
- AC "test validates the fetcher's snapshot output against that schema" → 8937 AC2.
- AC "test validates each differ's snapshot reads against that schema" → 8937 AC1+AC3 (schema covers every field the differs read; joint round-trip drives inbound+outbound differs reading the schema-validated snapshot).
- AC "Outbound collapsed to a single production path" + AC "retired outbound applier path deleted" + SC "outbound exercised through the production path, not a dead path" → 33d0.
- AC "outbound sub-op telemetry (links_applied/comments_applied/labels_applied) surfaced in the structured batch outcome" + AC "computed>0 && applied==0 fails loudly, after the warn-first rollout" + SC "silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary" → 2359.
- AC "bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip asserted hermetically through the production path" + AC "every expected-bug probe guard converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes" + SC "bidirectional round-trips asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected" → 822a (reuses fe57's fixtures; depends_on fe57).
- AC "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)" → 8937 (story B). The parent AC NAMES its owner verbatim; 8937's description ("Owns the epic's guardrail artifact … commit a documented anti-change-detector guardrail … a short docs/ note (ADR-style) … New tests reference it") and 8937 AC5 deliver it. Under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a NAMED consumer counts) this is FULLY covered.

ANTI-FP / WITHDRAWAL of the recurring AC6 false positive previously raised on this ticket and across the roster ("documented anti-change-detector guardrail only PARTIALLY covered — no sibling produces a governing artifact"): that finding is a FALSE POSITIVE and stays WITHDRAWN. It was raised against a paraphrased "governs new tests" wording that does not match the plan text; the actual parent AC reads verbatim "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)" — it explicitly NAMES its owner (story B = 8937) and is fully delivered by 8937's description + AC5. Sibling audits on 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937 have already withdrawn the same false positive.

4-bucket summary for this pairing:
- FULLY (fe57 owns its half; jointly complete): parent AC "round-trip contract uses fixtures derived from real Jira payloads" (fixtures → fe57; test → 8937).
- CONTRIBUTED: parent SC "producer/consumers cannot drift on snapshot shape" (honest-input half), parent SC "zero change-detector / lean suite" (re-capture cadence keeps fixtures from rotting into change-detectors).
- COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING: SSOT schema + fetcher/differ validation (8937); round-trip contract test (8937); applier collapse + retired-path deletion + production-path exercise (33d0); sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary (2359); bidirectional hermetic round-trip + probe de-encoding (822a); verified-fake fidelity (59ef); documented anti-change-detector guardrail (8937, story B — named owner).
- STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE: parent AC "all six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green."
- UNCOVERED: none.

No G3 coverage finding for this pairing. fe57's owned share (real-payload fixtures + unmassaged FakeAcliClient through the production path + capture/scrub script + re-capture doc) is the foundation that three siblings (8937, 59ef, 822a) explicitly depend_on; the union of children covers every parent acceptance/success criterion, and no parent criterion is left uncovered because of this child.
